message:
fix: link-prediction soundarajan_hopcroft + WIC raise correct types (br-r37-c1-fa5tf)

Three link-prediction functions raised the wrong exception type on
multigraph and directed inputs, plus the wrong exception class for
the delta<=0 precondition in within_inter_cluster.

Bug 1 (multigraph/directed type guard):
    >>> g = fnx.MultiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
    >>> list(fnx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(g))
    NetworkXAlgorithmError: No community information available for Node 1
    >>> list(nx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(nx.MultiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])))
    NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for multigraph type

The Rust path fell through to the per-node community-attr check
before any type guard fired, masking the real type incompatibility
with a misleading 'No community information available' error.

Affects: cn_soundarajan_hopcroft, ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft,
within_inter_cluster.

Bug 2 (within_inter_cluster delta precondition exception class):
    >>> fnx.within_inter_cluster(fnx.Graph([(1,2)]), delta=0)
    NetworkXError: Delta must be greater than zero
    >>> nx.within_inter_cluster(nx.Graph([(1,2)]), delta=0)
    NetworkXAlgorithmError: Delta must be greater than zero

nx raises NetworkXAlgorithmError (a subclass of NetworkXError, but
drop-in code that catches the algorithm-error class specifically
won't catch the plain parent).

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``:

For each of cn_soundarajan_hopcroft / ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft:
- Add eager type guard at the top of the function (before any
  community lookup) raising NetworkXNotImplemented for multigraph
  / directed inputs.
- Restructure the generator body into an inner ``_gen()`` returned
  from the outer function so the type check runs on call (matching
  nx's argmap-decorator behavior — synchronous, not iteration-time).

For within_inter_cluster:
- Add the same type guards before the existing delta check.
- Promote the delta check to ``NetworkXAlgorithmError`` to match
  nx's class.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_link_prediction_multigraph_guard_parity.py``:
- test_multigraph_raises_eagerly (parametrized over 3 funcs ×
  {MultiGraph, MultiDiGraph} = 6 cases; verifies the call itself
  raises, not iteration)
- test_digraph_raises_directed (3 cases)
- test_multigraph_caught_by_nx_class (drop-in catchability)
- test_simple_graph_unchanged (regression — community-aware path
  still works on Graph)
- test_within_inter_cluster_negative_delta_raises_algorithm_error
  (delta precondition class fix)
- test_multigraph_short_circuits_before_negative_delta_check
  (decorator ordering: when both delta<=0 AND multigraph, the
  type guard fires first matching nx)

Full sweep
----------
6774 passed, 0 failed (was 6761 before — +13 new regression cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -65,6 +65,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f64cy","title":"parity: pagerank silently falls back to unweighted when weight attr is missing","description":"fnx.pagerank(G, weight='nope') on a graph whose edges lack a 'nope' attribute returns the unweighted result instead of raising. NetworkX defaults the missing attribute to 1 (treated as edge weight 1) — semantically same as unweighted but only when ALL edges lack the key. fnx behavior is similar but should be verified against nx for partial-attribute graphs (some edges weighted, others missing). Add parity test that builds a graph where half the edges have weight, half don't, and asserts fnx and nx agree to 1e-9.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:26:41.408497679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:11:17.258068772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T09:11:17.256114832Z","close_reason":"Verified parity. fnx.pagerank already matches nx exactly on per-edge default-1 fallback for missing weight attributes — no code fix needed. Landed tests/python/test_pagerank_weight_parity.py (commit 400eefe) with 6 cases pinning the contract: all-weighted, weight='nope' (all missing), partial weights (half missing), weight=None, DiGraph partial, plus a sanity check that asymmetric weights produce a non-trivial pagerank delta vs unweighted (proving the implementation isn't in a hidden global fallback path).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f6epo","title":"parity: second_order_centrality dict iterates in wrong node order","description":"fnx.second_order_centrality returns a dict in arbitrary internal order (e.g. for path_graph(5): [2, 4, 0, 1, 3]) instead of nx's node-insertion order [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]. Same iteration-order drift as br-r37-c1-pm78h (communicability_betweenness_centrality). Drop-in code that iterates 'for n, score in centrality.items(): ...' breaks. Values are unchanged. Fix: reorder result dict in iteration order of G.nodes().","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:17:46.842301576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:19:51.423520818Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:19:51.421530578Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 58665d3. second_order_centrality now iterates in node-insertion order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f8gi1","title":"MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph.nodes() returns frozen list, not live NodeView","description":"fnx.MultiGraph.nodes() and fnx.MultiDiGraph.nodes() return a frozen list snapshot at call time. nx returns a live NodeView. Inconsistent with fnx.Graph.nodes() and fnx.DiGraph.nodes() which DO return live views.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.MultiGraph([(1,2)])
  >>> v = g.nodes()
  >>> g.add_node(99)
  >>> 99 in list(v)
  False                  # nx returns True

Root cause: the Rust-side MultiGraphNodeView.__call__ and MultiDiGraphNodeView.__call__ return list when called with default args (data=False). For Graph and DiGraph the Rust call returns self (a live NodeView) — only the multi-variants are wrong.

The Python wrapper _node_view_call_with_attr_support calls node_view_call(self, False, default) for data is False and trusts the Rust impl. Override that branch for Multi* classes to return self instead, matching the simple-graph branch.

Same class as br-r37-c1-msf5j (DiGraph/Multi*.edges() live view) — closes the parallel defect for nodes().","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:49:40.967497390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:54:44.642938229Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T04:54:44.640112396Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-fa5tf","title":"link-prediction soundarajan_hopcroft / within_inter_cluster raise wrong type on multigraph","description":"Three link-prediction functions raise NetworkXAlgorithmError('No community information available for Node 1') on multigraph input, masking the real issue. nx is decorated with @not_implemented_for('multigraph') and raises NetworkXNotImplemented FIRST, before any community-attribute check.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.MultiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])
  >>> list(fnx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(g))
  NetworkXAlgorithmError: No community information available for Node 1
  >>> list(nx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(nx.MultiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])))
  NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for multigraph type

Affects cn_soundarajan_hopcroft, ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft, within_inter_cluster — all share the same code path that checks community attrs before the type guard. Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXNotImplemented) won't trigger.

This is the link-prediction subset scoped out of br-r37-c1-tqimg. Fix: add multigraph guard at the top of each function, before any community-attribute lookup. Also add @not_implemented_for('directed') guards since nx decorates these functions with both.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T08:14:21.278147577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T08:14:52.279137656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fauol","title":"parity: ego_graph node ordering uses set iteration (random) instead of BFS-from-source order","description":"fnx.ego_graph node order is the iteration order of a set (arbitrary hash order). nx.ego_graph nodes are in BFS-from-source visit order (source first, then by distance from source). For node 'c' on graph with edges (c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e), radius=2: fnx returns ['d','e','a','c','b'] but nx returns ['c','d','a','b','e']. Drop-in code that relies on iteration order breaks. Fix: iterate the BFS 'seen' dict (which records insertion order) instead of converting to set.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:07:07.265713125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:10:10.734025927Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:10:10.732030928Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 76c3369. ego_graph now iterates nodes in G's insertion order (filtered to ego set) matching nx's G.subgraph contract. 10 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fs3bl","title":"min_weight_matching tuple direction drifts from nx","description":"fnx.min_weight_matching returns matched pairs with different tuple directions than nx (same family pattern as br-r37-c1-kpnc8 which fixed max_weight_matching).

Repro:
  edges = [(0,1),(0,2),(1,2),(2,3)]
  fnx -> {(0,1), (2,3)}
  nx  -> {(1,0), (3,2)}   <- tuple direction reversed

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]
  fnx -> {('a','c'), ('d','e')}
  nx  -> {('e','d'), ('a','c')}

The matching choice itself is correct (same nodes paired); only the per-pair tuple direction differs. Drop-in code that compared the matching against a reference set of (u,v) pairs broke.

Fix: delegate min_weight_matching to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity, mirroring br-r37-c1-kpnc8 (max_weight_matching). Preserve the existing weight= and MultiGraph-projection support.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:25:25.186247477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:28:02.128625491Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:28:02.126228636Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fuuor","title":"parity: random_labeled_rooted_forest stray q arg + tutte_polynomial positional x/y","description":"Two stale signatures: random_labeled_rooted_forest(n, q=None, seed=None) carried a stray q from random_unlabeled_rooted_forest (nx is just (n, *, seed=None)). tutte_polynomial(G, x=None, y=None) accepted x/y positionally as a numeric-evaluation extension; nx is (G,) only. Drop-in callers writing for nx and trying tutte_polynomial(G, 1, 1) had the call work on fnx but raise TypeError on nx. Aligned: rrf drops q (replaced by random_unlabeled_rooted_forest forwarder), tutte_polynomial makes x/y KEYWORD_ONLY. Also fixed tutte_polynomial recursion to use kwarg form internally. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:36:37.664864574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:37:22.476756359Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:37:22.474915674Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4b6c6de. random_labeled_rooted_forest signature is now (n, *, seed=None) matching nx; tutte_polynomial keeps the x/y numeric-evaluation extension but as KEYWORD_ONLY so positional usage rejects identically. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -21412,21 +21412,35 @@ def cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(G, ebunch=None, community="community"):
     community as None and silently computed a plain common-neighbor
     count without the bonus — a correctness trap for callers that
     assumed nx contract. Now raises the same error.
+
+    br-r37-c1-fa5tf: nx is @not_implemented_for('directed',
+    'multigraph'). Pre-fix fnx fell through to the per-node
+    community-attr check and raised NetworkXAlgorithmError instead
+    of NetworkXNotImplemented. Raise the type error eagerly (on
+    function call, matching nx's argmap behavior) so iteration
+    never reaches the community check on multi/directed inputs.
     """
-    if ebunch is None:
-        ebunch = non_edges(G)
-    for u, v in ebunch:
-        u_comm = _lp_community_of(G, u, community)
-        v_comm = _lp_community_of(G, v, community)
-        u_nbrs = set(G.neighbors(u))
-        v_nbrs = set(G.neighbors(v))
-        common = u_nbrs & v_nbrs
-        score = len(common)
-        if u_comm == v_comm:
-            for w in common:
-                if _lp_community_of(G, w, community) == u_comm:
-                    score += 1
-        yield (u, v, score)
+    if G.is_multigraph():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for multigraph type")
+    if G.is_directed():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for directed type")
+
+    def _gen():
+        eb = non_edges(G) if ebunch is None else ebunch
+        for u, v in eb:
+            u_comm = _lp_community_of(G, u, community)
+            v_comm = _lp_community_of(G, v, community)
+            u_nbrs = set(G.neighbors(u))
+            v_nbrs = set(G.neighbors(v))
+            common = u_nbrs & v_nbrs
+            score = len(common)
+            if u_comm == v_comm:
+                for w in common:
+                    if _lp_community_of(G, w, community) == u_comm:
+                        score += 1
+            yield (u, v, score)
+
+    return _gen()
 
 
 def ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft(G, ebunch=None, community="community"):
@@ -21437,28 +21451,38 @@ def ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft(G, ebunch=None, community="community"):
     neighbors w whose community == Cu, when Cu==Cv; else 0. fnx's
     previous formula added a per-w bonus term that was never part of
     the nx definition, producing systematically larger scores.
+
+    br-r37-c1-fa5tf: same multigraph/directed eager-raise pattern as
+    cn_soundarajan_hopcroft. See that function for details.
     """
-    if ebunch is None:
-        ebunch = non_edges(G)
-    for u, v in ebunch:
-        u_comm = _lp_community_of(G, u, community)
-        v_comm = _lp_community_of(G, v, community)
-        if u_comm != v_comm:
-            # br-shratype: nx returns int 0 here (no division on early
-            # return); match exactly so type-sensitive callers don't
-            # diverge.
-            yield (u, v, 0)
-            continue
-        u_nbrs = set(G.neighbors(u))
-        v_nbrs = set(G.neighbors(v))
-        common = u_nbrs & v_nbrs
-        score = 0
-        for w in common:
-            if _lp_community_of(G, w, community) == u_comm:
-                deg_w = G.degree(w)
-                if deg_w > 0:
-                    score = score + 1 / deg_w
-        yield (u, v, score)
+    if G.is_multigraph():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for multigraph type")
+    if G.is_directed():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for directed type")
+
+    def _gen():
+        eb = non_edges(G) if ebunch is None else ebunch
+        for u, v in eb:
+            u_comm = _lp_community_of(G, u, community)
+            v_comm = _lp_community_of(G, v, community)
+            if u_comm != v_comm:
+                # br-shratype: nx returns int 0 here (no division on early
+                # return); match exactly so type-sensitive callers don't
+                # diverge.
+                yield (u, v, 0)
+                continue
+            u_nbrs = set(G.neighbors(u))
+            v_nbrs = set(G.neighbors(v))
+            common = u_nbrs & v_nbrs
+            score = 0
+            for w in common:
+                if _lp_community_of(G, w, community) == u_comm:
+                    deg_w = G.degree(w)
+                    if deg_w > 0:
+                        score = score + 1 / deg_w
+            yield (u, v, score)
+
+    return _gen()
 
 
 def node_attribute_xy(G, attribute, nodes=None):
@@ -32494,9 +32518,20 @@ def within_inter_cluster(G, ebunch=None, delta=0.001, community="community"):
     If u and v are in different communities, score is 0.
     Otherwise, score = |within| / (|inter| + delta) where within are common
     neighbors in the same community as u/v, and inter are those in different.
+
+    br-r37-c1-fa5tf: nx is @not_implemented_for('directed',
+    'multigraph'). Type guard fires eagerly (on call) so iteration
+    never reaches the per-node community-attr check on multi/
+    directed inputs.
     """
+    if G.is_multigraph():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for multigraph type")
+    if G.is_directed():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for directed type")
     if delta <= 0:
-        raise NetworkXError("Delta must be greater than zero")
+        # br-r37-c1-fa5tf: nx raises NetworkXAlgorithmError (not
+        # NetworkXError) for this precondition; match exactly.
+        raise NetworkXAlgorithmError("Delta must be greater than zero")
 
     if ebunch is None:
         # Default: all non-existing edges.

@@ -0,0 +1,168 @@
+"""Parity for link-prediction soundarajan_hopcroft / within_inter_cluster
+on multigraph and directed inputs.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-fa5tf. Three link-prediction functions raised
+``NetworkXAlgorithmError('No community information available for
+Node 1')`` on multigraph input — masking the real issue. nx is
+decorated with ``@not_implemented_for('directed', 'multigraph')``
+and raises ``NetworkXNotImplemented`` first, before any community-
+attribute lookup.
+
+This is a follow-up to br-r37-c1-tqimg which scoped these out
+because the existing fnx wrappers fell through to a per-node
+community check before any type guard.
+
+Affected functions:
+- ``cn_soundarajan_hopcroft``
+- ``ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft``
+- ``within_inter_cluster``
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+LP_FNS = [
+    "cn_soundarajan_hopcroft",
+    "ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft",
+    "within_inter_cluster",
+]
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Eager raise on multigraph / directed inputs
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("fn_name", LP_FNS)
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+def test_multigraph_raises_eagerly(fn_name, cls_name):
+    """Type check fires on call (not on iteration). nx's argmap
+    decorator runs synchronously, so calling ``fn(G)`` on a
+    MultiGraph raises immediately, before any iteration."""
+    G = getattr(fnx, cls_name)([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = getattr(nx, cls_name)([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(
+        fnx.NetworkXNotImplemented,
+        match=r"not implemented for multigraph type",
+    ):
+        # The call itself must raise — don't iterate.
+        getattr(fnx, fn_name)(G)
+    with pytest.raises(
+        nx.NetworkXNotImplemented,
+        match=r"not implemented for multigraph type",
+    ):
+        getattr(nx, fn_name)(GX)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("fn_name", LP_FNS)
+def test_digraph_raises_directed(fn_name):
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(
+        fnx.NetworkXNotImplemented,
+        match=r"not implemented for directed type",
+    ):
+        getattr(fnx, fn_name)(G)
+    with pytest.raises(
+        nx.NetworkXNotImplemented,
+        match=r"not implemented for directed type",
+    ):
+        getattr(nx, fn_name)(GX)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Cross-class catching
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_multigraph_caught_by_nx_class():
+    """Drop-in: a fnx-raised NetworkXNotImplemented must be
+    catchable via ``except nx.NetworkXNotImplemented``."""
+    G = fnx.MultiGraph([(1, 2)])
+    try:
+        fnx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(G)
+    except nx.NetworkXNotImplemented:
+        return
+    pytest.fail(
+        "fnx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft should raise NetworkXNotImplemented "
+        "on MultiGraph"
+    )
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regression: simple Graph behavior unchanged
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_simple_graph_unchanged():
+    """The community-attribute-aware path must continue to work for
+    simple Graph inputs with community attrs set."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+    for n in (1, 2):
+        G.nodes[n]["community"] = 0
+        GX.nodes[n]["community"] = 0
+    for n in (3, 4):
+        G.nodes[n]["community"] = 1
+        GX.nodes[n]["community"] = 1
+
+    # cn_soundarajan_hopcroft: same output as nx
+    f = sorted(
+        [(u, v, round(s, 4)) for u, v, s in fnx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(G, [(1, 3)])]
+    )
+    n = sorted(
+        [(u, v, round(s, 4)) for u, v, s in nx.cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(GX, [(1, 3)])]
+    )
+    assert f == n
+
+    # within_inter_cluster: same output
+    f = sorted(
+        [(u, v, round(s, 4)) for u, v, s in fnx.within_inter_cluster(G, [(1, 3)])]
+    )
+    n = sorted(
+        [(u, v, round(s, 4)) for u, v, s in nx.within_inter_cluster(GX, [(1, 3)])]
+    )
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_within_inter_cluster_negative_delta_raises_algorithm_error():
+    """``delta <= 0`` raises ``NetworkXAlgorithmError`` (not
+    ``NetworkXError``) — matching nx exactly. Pre-fix fnx raised the
+    plain ``NetworkXError`` parent class. Drop-in code that catches
+    ``NetworkXAlgorithmError`` would not trigger on the parent."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(
+        fnx.NetworkXAlgorithmError, match=r"Delta must be greater than zero"
+    ):
+        fnx.within_inter_cluster(G, delta=0)
+    with pytest.raises(
+        nx.NetworkXAlgorithmError, match=r"Delta must be greater than zero"
+    ):
+        nx.within_inter_cluster(GX, delta=0)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_multigraph_short_circuits_before_negative_delta_check():
+    """Drop-in: when both delta <= 0 AND multigraph, the multigraph
+    guard must fire FIRST (matching nx's argmap-decorator order)."""
+    G = fnx.MultiGraph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.MultiGraph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXNotImplemented, match=r"multigraph"):
+        fnx.within_inter_cluster(G, delta=-1)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXNotImplemented, match=r"multigraph"):
+        nx.within_inter_cluster(GX, delta=-1)
